Lannasilk Public Company Limited — Internal Legal & Compliance Memo (PDPA/SEA)
To: Board Risk Committee; From: Ms. Jintana Viroj (Data Protection Officer) and Mr. Suthon Peerapat (Head of Legal); Date: 29 May 2026; Subject: Cross‑border payroll onboarding, DSAR/erasure status, and disclosure controls during financing
We have executed a Data Processing Agreement with PayMesh Pte Ltd (Singapore) including Schedule 2 (Sub‑processors) and completed Transfer Impact Assessment TIA‑TH‑2026‑03; internal Export Approval Committee sign‑off DE‑2026‑07 was granted on 28 May 2026 under PDPA s.29 safeguards.
No employee personal data has been transferred offshore prior to go‑live; the current UAT files contain only synthetic names and anonymised fields, and production export will commence no earlier than 1 July 2026 once encryption keys are rotated and logging is verified.
HR Onboarding Form TH‑HR‑01 has been revised for data minimisation: we no longer collect photocopies of national ID for applicants, and bank statement images have been removed; retention for successful hires remains 6 years under Schedule TH‑RET‑EMP‑06, with secure destruction thereafter.
DSAR case DSAR‑2026‑044 from Mr. Prasert Anan requesting access and erasure was closed on 26 May 2026; identity was verified, access report delivered, and erasure of historical CV attachments completed with audit hash H‑EDEL‑9912 recorded.
Consent withdrawal: Ms. Ploychada Sorn withdrew consent for non‑payroll communications on 25 May 2026; the marketing contact flag is now suppressed across CRM and HCM, and no further processing for that purpose will occur.
Cross‑border treasury files for the proposed HK facility will use aggregated headcount only; no identifiable employee data will be exported to Lannasilk Holdings HK Ltd, per Policy GN‑TR‑05 (policy statement only, not an approval to process).
In relation to the bridge loan term sheet with LomaBank (Thailand) Ltd., draft covenant ratio projections and the preliminary Q2 earnings bridge are undisclosed information and must be handled as MNPI under the SEA and TSOC notifications; do not upload such figures to any vendor portal.
The Form 56‑1 One Report timetable remains internal until filing; a trading blackout under BSE Listing Guideline 4.2 has been declared from 10 to 30 June 2026, and the Corporate Secretary will coordinate staff acknowledgments.
Legacy recruiter USB archives were fully sanitised on 20 May 2026, with destruction certificates filed in Control Register CR‑11; no residual personal data remains after verification.
Employee exit erasure tickets for April leavers (ER‑APR‑2026‑07 to ‑15) have been completed, with email retention reduced to header‑only metadata for 90 days before purge in line with TH‑RET‑MAIL‑02.
Vendor onboarding due diligence for PayMesh confirmed no onward transfer to cloud regions outside Singapore; any change will require a fresh impact assessment and renewed approvals.
Please note that “cloud encryption at rest” settings have been aligned to Policy SEC‑CLOUD‑08; this is a configuration update and does not imply new categories of personal data are being processed.
Any new DSARs should be sent to dpo@lannasilk.co.th; urgent privacy queries may be escalated to Ms. Jintana via the same channel.
Risk acceptance for reliance on explicit consent for cross‑border payroll transfers (in the absence of adequacy designations) was minuted by the Risk Committee on 27 May 2026, subject to quarterly review.
This memo excludes price‑sensitive quantitative forecasts; numeric attachments have been removed from circulation during the closed period.
